title: campanula cephalaria var. macrophylla
intro: Campanula cephalaria var. macrophylla, also known as Bellflower or Harebell, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Campanulaceae family and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant properties. The health benefits of Campanula cephalaria var. macrophylla include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce stress and improve overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
